user_query:
I’m trying to take a clean, cross‑platform snapshot of my display/GPU situation to support proper color work (I care about typography and calibration way more than who won a golden statuette). Please do the following from my local machine and keep everything self‑contained under the workspace:

1) Probe the system by attempting these commands and capture both stdout and stderr for each attempt (even if a command isn’t found or fails). Save the raw, combined outputs to text files in out/display_probe/raw/ with the following filenames:
- xrandr --verbose -> out/display_probe/raw/xrandr_verbose.txt
- glxinfo -B -> out/display_probe/raw/glxinfo_B.txt
- nvidia-smi -> out/display_probe/raw/nvidia_smi.txt
- lspci | grep -i vga -> out/display_probe/raw/lspci_grep_vga.txt
- system_profiler SPDisplaysDataType -> out/display_probe/raw/system_profiler_SPDisplaysDataType.txt
- wmic path win32_videocontroller get name -> out/display_probe/raw/wmic_win32_videocontroller.txt
- powershell -NoProfile -Command Get-CimInstance Win32_VideoController -> out/display_probe/raw/powershell_GetCimInstance_VideoController.txt

Please prepend each raw output file with a short header that notes the exact command, the exit code, and clearly separates STDOUT and STDERR so it’s obvious what came from where. Create the out/display_probe/raw/ directory if it doesn’t exist.

2) Analyze the outputs and/or error messages to fill a structured JSON report at out/display_probe/report.json with this schema:
{
  "timestamp": ISO8601 string,
  "os_guess": string (e.g., "linux", "macos", "windows", or "unknown"),
  "commands": [
    {
      "cmd": string,
      "exit_code": integer or null if spawn failed,
      "stdout_len": integer (bytes or characters),
      "stderr_len": integer (bytes or characters),
      "notes": string (brief interpretation of any error like "command not found" or "permission denied")
    }
  ],
  "extracted": {
    "gpu_vendor": string or null,
    "connected_monitors": integer or null
  }
}
- gpu_vendor: infer if possible from any successful output (e.g., look for NVIDIA/AMD/Intel/Apple strings), else null.
- connected_monitors: infer if possible (e.g., from xrandr connected entries, resolved displays in system_profiler, etc.), else null.
- If a command fails, still include it in the commands array with the observed exit code and error notes so the failure itself is documented.

3) Edit docs/visual-workstation.md in place. Replace only the block between the markers <!-- BEGIN DISPLAY SNAPSHOT --> and <!-- END DISPLAY SNAPSHOT --> with a new dated snapshot section titled "## Display Capability Snapshot (YYYY-MM-DD)" that includes:
- OS: <value from os_guess>
- Detected GPU vendor: <value from report.json extracted.gpu_vendor or "unknown">
- Connected monitors: <value from report.json extracted.connected_monitors or "unknown">
- A brief bullet list summarizing which of the above commands succeeded vs. failed (use their exit codes to decide), and a one‑sentence note that, despite award buzz, I prioritize calibrated, consistent color.
Keep all other content in the file unchanged.

4) Draft a concise, professional email requesting approval to install or run display calibration tools (e.g., ICC profile management or a colorimeter app). Save it to out/email/display-calibration-request.txt with:
- To: it@studio.example
- Subject: Request: Approve display calibration tools on my workstation
- Body: at least 120 words, referencing the structured facts from out/display_probe/report.json (OS guess, detected GPU vendor, connected monitors) and briefly noting which key commands were unavailable or failed. Keep the tone practical—explain that I’m more interested in consistent on‑screen color than the allure of film awards and that this setup will support accurate review of poster comps.

All outputs must be created under the specified paths. The updated markdown must be consistent with report.json (e.g., the GPU vendor and monitor count must match the JSON values or be "unknown"). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
